author: NETWORG
name: Power Platform: Environment Variable Definition
description: Adds an environment variable definition (environmentvariabledefinition.xml). Supports Type: String, Number, Boolean, JSON, Secret (Azure Key Vault), ConnectionReference. Registers RootComponent type 380 in Solution.xml. Use pp-environment-variable-value to add the value override.

SolutionRootPath.description: Relative path from the project folder (.csproj) to the folder containing unpacked Dataverse solution files (Other/, Entities/, OptionSets/). Use '.' if solution files are directly in the project folder, or a subfolder name like 'Declarations'. Do not use 'Entities' or 'Other' — those are subdirectories inside the solution metadata root.

PublisherPrefix.description: Use the same publisher prefix as defined in Other/Solution.xml of the solution where this environment variable is introduced (e.g., tom, talxis).

LogicalName.description: Logical name of the environment variable without publisher prefix (e.g., portal_hostnamemain). The prefix is added automatically.

DisplayName.description: Display name for the environment variable that will be shown to users.

Description.description: Description shown in the environment variable details. If left empty, the <description> element is removed from the XML.

Type.description: Data type of the environment variable. String/Number/Boolean/JSON are plain-text values; Secret stores the value in Azure Key Vault (secretstore=1); ConnectionReference is a DataSource type for connection references.

choices.description: Yes/No value. Use DefaultValue='yes' or 'no'. type=100000002, secretstore=0.
Azure Key Vault secret reference. type=100000000, secretstore=1.
Data source / connection reference. type=100000004, secretstore=0. Connection-specific configuration is managed via the connectionreference component, not here.

DefaultValue.description: Default value shipped with the solution. For Boolean use 'yes' or 'no'. For JSON use a valid JSON object. If left empty, the <defaultvalue> element is removed from the XML.

IsRequired.description: Whether the environment variable must have a value at solution import time.

choices.description: Required — solution import fails if no value is supplied.

SchemaName.description: Final environment variable schema/logical name including publisher prefix (automatically generated).

HasDefaultValue.description: Automatically computed: True when DefaultValue is non-empty.

HasDescription.description: Automatically computed: True when Description is non-empty.

manualInstructions.text: Registering environment variable definition in Solution.xml

postActions.description: Registering environment variable definition in Solution.xml
